name: Billing contact details (email and phone)
pageTitle: Change billing contact details - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Billing
heading: Change billing contact details

headingVariants.note: The heading/page title is computed at request time by getPageTitle: 'Add' when BOTH email and phoneNumber are absent, 'Change' otherwise. The trace captured the 'Change' wording because the flow always arrives via the 'Change email address' link once billing details are populated. This resolves the earlier open question about whether a non-'Change' variant exists — it does ('Add').
headingVariants.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/routes/handlers/importer/billing_contact_details.js:89-94 (getPageTitle returns 'Add' when !email && !phoneNumber, else 'Change'); page title assembled at lines 52-53

structure.text: We'll send the invoice to this email address

govukComponents.usage: Email address (type=text) and Phone number (type=tel, autocomplete=tel) inputs — both carry govuk-!-width-three-quarters
Field labels for Email address and Phone number
Hint text under each label (email-hint, telephone-hint)
Wrapping each field (label + hint + input). In an error state gains an error modifier via {{displayErrorClass errors 'email'|'telephone'}}
Validation summary rendered above the page title on a failed submit (common/validationSummary partial). No trace rendered it — no test drives an error state on this page.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.hint: We'll send the invoice to this email address
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — Joi schema declares [EMAIL] .required() in ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/billing_contact_details_validator.js:23-29. This is the old system's mandatoriness; the rebuild may revisit whether billing email is mandatory here, but IPAFFS enforced it.

constraints.format: email — Joi .email({ ignoreLength: true, tlds: { allow: false } }), so TLD list is not checked

fields.conditionalOn: CUC (Common User Charge) notifications only — this page is reached only when isCuc=true, via the 'Change email address' link on the Confirm billing details page (ched-d-workflows.ts:355-361, gated by the isCuc flag; DEFAULT_CONFIG.isCuc defaults to false at line 143)

Observed value corroborated by DEFAULT_CONFIG.billingEmail = 'email@mailinator.com' (ched-d-workflows.ts:146). openQuestions: VALIDATION now sourced from legacy (was a gap): both email and telephone are server-side mandatory in IPAFFS (Joi .required()), with full format/length rules and error copy captured in validationMessages. No trace rendered these (the sole CUC test fills both with valid values and asserts nothing about errors), so all six messages are confidence=legacy, not confirmed. The rebuild should decide whether to carry forward IPAFFS mandatoriness and these exact constraints (email max 100, phone 9-20 chars, phone char-set 0-9 / + ().-).

HEADING variant resolved from legacy: the page title/H1 is 'Add billing contact details' first time (both fields empty) and 'Change billing contact details' thereafter — computed by getPageTitle (billing_contact_details.js:89-94). The trace only ever showed 'Change' because the flow arrives via the change link with details already populated. No conflict with the rendered value; recorded as headingVariants.

The page object (BillingChangeContactDetailsPage.ts) exposes exactly the two fields already in this spec (Email address, Phone number) plus the Continue button — corroborated complete against both the QA repo and the legacy template (billingContactDetails.html has no further inputs beyond email + telephone + hidden crumb/etag).

Back link href was '#' (JS-driven history back) in the snapshot, not a concrete URL. The test-observed predecessor is the Confirm billing details page (ConfirmBillingDetailsPage), reached via its linkChangeEmailAddress (#confirm-billing-address-email); the successor after a valid POST is CONFIRM_BILLING_DETAILS_PAGE (billing_contact_details.js:36-40). So this page is a sub-detour off the Confirm billing details page, not a linear journey step — consistent with journey position -1.

This page sits inside a multi-step CUC billing sub-flow: Confirm billing details -> (change address) Find an address -> Select the address -> (change email) THIS page -> back to Confirm billing details -> Save and continue (ched-d-workflows.ts:355-361). The sibling billing pages (find-an-address, billing-select-the-address, confirm-billing-details) are separate page specs; this one covers only the contact (email + phone) leg.

PREFILL behaviour (legacy, informational): if the notification has no billing email/phone yet, or ?source=IDM, the handler prefills email/phone from the trader's IDM/CUC account billing details (billing_contact_details.js:62-87, cucHelper.getBillingDetails). Not a field on this page per se, but explains where the 'Change' path's initial values come from.
